You can install the development version of metacheck from [GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
pak::pkg_install("scienceverse/metacheck")
```




You may not contribute any code unless you also contribute a test of this code.

recent_commits.message: The OpenScales OSD export now exists end-to-end. Work spanned three files:
recent_commits.body:
R/data_check_helpers.R — two shared, package-level helpers (so both the module and the converter can call them):

.osd_safe_code() — spec-valid code sanitizer.
.osd_code_and_provenance() — the three-tier provenance/code minting (dictionary → bare official code or curated METACHECK-; manuscript → METACHECK-; self-generated → METACHECK-GEN-), each with a human-readable provenance sentence.
inst/modules/codebook_check.R — rewrote .scales_to_osd(scale_groups, columns_df, labels_df, dict):

Spec-conformant structure: osd_version, definition.{scale_info, likert_options, items}, translations.en, plus a namespaced definition.metacheck block for provenance/confidence/source (kept out of the spec objects).
likert_options lets the codebook lead — decodes value_labels for range + anchor labels (source:"codebook"); falls back to observed min/max/n_unique from data_check stats (source:"observed"); omitted entirely when nothing is scale-shaped (no invention).
translations.en carries codebook item wording; text_key points at it per spec.
Named groups flagged write=TRUE; unnamed detections write=FALSE (report-only, no file).
R/psychds-convert.R — the file writer + cross-reference:

.osd_write_scales() writes one scales/{CODE}/{CODE}.osd per named scale (pretty JSON) under the dataset root.
.osd_write_readme_section() appends a "Psychometric scales" section to the root README.md explaining the three provenance markers and listing every scale (your "general README" decision).
Added metacheck:scale code + source to variableMeasured, so Psych-DS variables cross-reference their OSD definition.
n_scales added to the return list and completion message.

recent_commits.message: fix(llm): correct ollama-native gate (&& not %%); fail-fast param/model checks
recent_commits.body:
- llm(): the ollama-native branch used `%%` (modulo) where `&&` was intended,
  so use_ollama_native came from arithmetic on logicals rather than a boolean
  AND. Fixed to `&&`.
- Validate `params` and the presence of a model up front, before any
  provider/network work, with clear error messages.
- llm_model(): use missing() so llm_model(NULL) can reset the option
  (a saved NULL round-trips); regen man/llm_model.Rd to match.

recent_commits.message: feat(plumber): bibr-JSON REST API — validated uploads, HTML report, hardened runtime
recent_commits.body:
Rework of the Plumber API around bibr JSON ingestion, extracted from #329:
- validate uploads as bibr JSON; raise size cap to 50MB
- read via metacheck:::.read_bibr (surfaces per-file errors that read() swallows)
- env-driven LLM config; /check includes metacheck_version and can render
  metacheck's HTML report (report_html); report=false to skip
- DRY endpoint pipeline, add /modules endpoint, strip unserializable S3
  classes from JSON responses
- hardened Docker runtime + docker-compose; expanded README
- new tests: test-api, test-plumber-helpers, test-plumber-validators

recent_commits.message: feat(read): let bibr abstract and figure/table captions flow through
recent_commits.body:
read() dropped info$abstract and figure/table $caption via three
"tempfix" NULL assignments left over from before bibr emitted them.
bibr now provides these fields, so stop discarding them. paper_validate()
does not require these columns and .paper_coerce() leaves unknown columns
untouched, so this is additive on the current schema and pairs with the
caption $defs in the v10.5 schema sync (#330).

Extracted from #329.

recent_commits.author: Jakub Werner

recent_commits.message: fix(grobid): stop anchor normalization from corrupting paper text
recent_commits.body:
grobid_to_bibr() rewrote every occurrence of each URL's anchor text with
its href across the whole paper. Short anchors (e.g. "Fig") clobbered
unrelated matches like "Fig. 2", and NA anchors were iterated blindly.
Restrict the substitution to anchors that ARE the URL (modulo spaces and
protocol), and skip NAs.

Extracted from #329 as a standalone fix.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01YLqyRiqzyaZncJ3m5VcjxK
recent_commits.author: Jakub Werner

recent_commits.message: fix(schema): sync bundled paper.json to bibr v10.5
recent_commits.body:
The published schema at scienceverse.org/schema/paper.json moved to
v10.5 (scienceverse/schema#1), which made test-paper.R's `.paper_schema`
test — an exact-equality check of the bundled schema against the
published contract — fail on every branch.

Sync the bundled inst/schema/paper.json to v10.5 (adds $defs funding,
affiliation, info_match, table.caption and many new info fields), with
ONE deliberate relaxation: drop `schema_version` from info.required.
metacheck must validate historical papers (psychsci, demopaper, user
PDFs) produced by pre-10.5 bibr, which emit bibr_version but not
schema_version; paper_validate() hard-errors on missing required
columns, so a byte-exact sync would break ~30 validation assertions.
Fresh paper() objects still gain all 27 info columns because the
constructor builds columns from properties, not required.

Update `.paper_schema` to pin schema_version as the only relaxed field
(and assert bundled adds nothing the remote lacks), then compare
everything else for exact drift — so unintended divergence is caught.

Verified: full suite green (0 failures, 2724 passed) on both dev and
jw_fable_fixes bases; `.paper_schema` passes online against live remote.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01YLqyRiqzyaZncJ3m5VcjxK
recent_commits.author: Jakub Werner

Full suite after this series: 0 failures, 2400 passes (previously

recent_commits.body:
Drop the three defensive deletions (info$abstract, figure$caption,
table$caption). Captions are plain nullable strings in every bibr
vintage that emits them, and 10.5 emits abstract as a nullable scalar;
verified against 10.0 demo, 10.2 golden, and real 10.5 outputs (info
stays one row in all three). Answers the open question in
scienceverse/schema#1.

recent_commits.message: plumber: DRY endpoint pipeline, /modules endpoint, report=false, hardened Docker runtime
recent_commits.body:
Centralise the per-endpoint upload boilerplate in with_uploaded_paper()
(single multipart parse, no second file copy, tempfile cleanup, optional
prevalidate hook that rejects bad params before the bibr parse). Add
GET /paper/modules discovery, a report=false switch on /check to skip
the Quarto render, and parse_bool() for multipart string flags.

Pin the Quarto version in the Dockerfile, run the compose service
read-only with a /tmp tmpfs, and pass LLM env config through.

The restricted parser set c("multi", "octet") is kept and documented:
"none" empirically breaks mime::parse_multipart (all uploads 500),
while the plumber defaults would JSON-parse the uploaded file a second
time. test-api.R now honours METACHECK_API_URL so tests can target a
non-default instance.

recent_commits.message: Transparent resource caps, LLM/download controls, data-manifest + archive tooling
recent_commits.body:
Replace silent truncation across the data pipeline with an upfront,
all-or-nothing "cannot complete as configured" gate that always names the
parameter and the value needed to proceed. Add interactive/auto cap prompts,
a per-paper file manifest, an OSF listing efficiency fix, and two crash fixes.

Caps & transparency
- New shared message builders cap_gate_size / cap_gate_count / cap_gate_unknown
  (R/report-helpers.R): a cap that would prevent fully completing a unit of work
  refuses that repository/codebook and reports the offending files/sizes, the
  parameter, and the lift-to value — no partial processing.
- download_repo_files (R/repo-download.R): upfront per-repo size gate; resolve
  NA-sized files via a HEAD Content-Length probe before downloading (fixes the
  bug where unsized files bypassed both caps); refuse repos with undeterminable
  sizes; defaults raised to max_file_size = 100 MB, max_download_size = 500 MB;
  "omitted" attribute replaced by "gated".
- data_check / code_check: file_limit is now an upfront per-repo gate (skip the
  whole repo, name file_limit + the true count) instead of a silent slice_head;
  same size-cap defaults; docs updated.
- codebook_check: codebook_max_calls param; codebook-parse and scale-ID tiers
  gate upfront instead of truncating silently; llm_max_calls() error now states
  the value to set.
- data_validate: max_facets promoted to an argument with a transparent
  "showing N of M" note (display cap, not a resource refusal).

Interactive cap control
- New auto() accessor + metacheck.auto option (default !interactive()) and
  cap_prompt() (R/cap-prompt.R): when interactive and not auto, a cap pauses and
  shows the offending files + sizes, letting the user skip or raise the limit;
  under auto() or a non-interactive session it reports inline and skips without
  blocking. Cap messages are emitted inline as they happen (not batched at the
  end of a loop). Wired through the download, file_limit, and codebook LLM caps.

Download modes & manifest
- data_check gains download = "data" (default; readable files only) / "all"
  (every repo file, for building a data archive) / "none"; TRUE/FALSE accepted
  as synonyms.
- data_check gains manifest = <path>: writes a per-paper JSON manifest (every
  file with URL, size, type, download status, and skip reason). NA-sized wanted
  files are HEAD-probed so sizes are complete for choosing an archive size
  ceiling. An empty manifest is written for papers with no accessible repository.
- convert_psychds: the opaque "N file(s) skipped (no local copy)" message now
  says how many of how many, why (not downloaded), a breakdown by file type, and
  how to include them (download = "all"); genuine copy failures are reported
  separately.

OSF efficiency
- osf_get_all_pages requests page[size]=100 (was the API default of 10),
  reducing listing calls up to ~10x and preventing OSF rate-limiting. Test
  fixtures resolved via an httptest2 redactor that strips the param on mock
  lookup.

Crash fixes
- data_analysis_unit: guard an all-NA identifier column (0/0 -> NaN in an if()).
- repo_check: guard NA file sizes in the human-readable size column
  (format.object_size errored on NA).

Progress bars
- llm() takes a phase label shown (with the model) in the progress bar, so LLM
  passes are identifiable; report_module_run attributes elapsed time to the
  running module instead of the next one.

Also: removed the col_type-only backward-compat path in convert_psychds() and
its test; added regression tests for the crash fixes, the cap gates, the
manifest, and the codebook LLM gate. Rewrote _stuff/run_openmind.R as a two-pass
(manifest sweep -> build) archive script.

repos.readme_excerpt:
`statcheck` is a “spellchecker” for statistics. It checks whether your
*p*-values match their accompanying test statistic and degrees of
freedom.

`statcheck` searches for null-hypothesis significance test (NHST) in APA
style (e.g., *t*(28) = 2.2, *p* \< .05). It recalculates the p-value
using the reported test statistic and degrees of freedom. If the
reported and computed p-values don’t match, `statcheck` will flag the
result as an error.

![](man/figures/infograph.png)


`statcheck` is mainly useful for:

1.  **Self-checks**: you can use `statcheck` to make sure your

[![Codecov test
coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/lhdjung/scrutiny/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://app.codecov.io/gh/lhdjung/scrutiny?branch=main)




The goal of scrutiny is to test published summary statistics for
consistency using techniques like GRIM and to check their plausibility.
The package makes these methods easy to use in a tidyverse-friendly way.
It hopes to help the new field of error detection go mainstream.

You can use the most important parts in the [Error
detection](https://errors.shinyapps.io/scrutiny) Shiny app instead.

repos.readme_excerpt:
PDF, DOCX, and HTML text extraction plus normalization for academic papers.

The full documentation lives in `docs/README.md`.


```bash
pip install docpluck
```

Optional extras:

```bash
pip install docpluck[docx]
pip install docpluck[html]
pip install docpluck[all]
```


`extract_pdf()` requires the `pdftotext` binary from Poppler.

- Linux/WSL: `apt-get install poppler-utils`
- macOS: `brew install poppler`
- Windows: install Poppler and add its `bin` folder to `PATH`

## Links

- Full usage and API reference: `docs/README.md`
- Normalization pipeline details: `docs/NORMALIZATION.md`
- Ben

recent_commits.body:
W0l — the two ×-as-3 glyph residuals the single-line W0k word-pair regex
structurally cannot reach (both silently render `3` where a scientist should
read `×`), documented in docs/FINDINGS_2026-07-03_efendic_glyph_in_prose_channel.md:

(A) recover_times_design_notation — factorial-design notation `<digit>(…) 3
    <digit>(…)`: "2 (Between-subject factor--Direction: …) 3 2 (…) 3 3
    (Within-subject factor--…) mixed-subject design" → each `) 3 <digit> (`
    boundary's `3` is a corrupted `×` (trailing digit is the real factor SIZE).
    Fires ONLY when tied to a genuine factor parenthetical: an adjacent
    parenthetical names a factor type, OR both sides are factor-size parens
    closed by a factorial/design tail. A range recode `(min 1) 3 2 (max 5)`,
    a formula `f(x) 3 2 (y)`, `(from level 3) 3 2 (see note)` are left alone.

(B) recover_times_wrapped_interaction — a line ending `<Pred> 3` whose next
    line starts with a predictor word, inside an "interaction" context:
    "(Direction 3\nManipulated Attribute × CMA)" → "Direction × Manipulated…".
    Reference-word tail guard (FIGURE 3 / Table 3 / Model 3) — the wide FP
    scan caught amj_1 `FIGURE 3\nRegression…` + jamison `Table 3\nMain-effects
    and interactions.` wrongly joined; guard added + regression-tested.

NORMALIZATION_VERSION 1.9.42 → 1.9.43. Wired channel 1 (normalize_text) AND
channel 3 (render post-process) per glyph-fixes-need-all-three-text-channels.

Verification (ground truth = AI multimodal reading golds via article-finder):
- Independent Sonnet canary audit CONFIRMS both efendic glyph fixes correct
  (design `× 2 × 3`, interaction `Direction × Manipulated Attribute × CMA`),
  no ×-as-3 / minus-as-2 corruption anywhere else in efendic.
- Wide 101-PDF raw-text FP scan CLEAN (0 non-target FPs; efendic recovered).
- 26-paper baseline 26/26 PASS. Normalize idempotency corpus PASS.
- 42 W0k/W0l tests + real-PDF end-to-end (fails at HEAD v2.4.115, passes here).

Resolves efendic's two ×-as-3 residuals. Remaining efendic findings (2 demoted

recent_commits.message: fix(render): strip affiliation remnant orphaned in Abstract body (chandrashekar) + v2.4.115
recent_commits.body:
A canary AI-verify found chandrashekar_2023_mp rendering "Department of Philosophy,
Lake Forest College" + "*Joint first authors" right after the ## Abstract heading,
before the abstract prose. The paper has 3 author affiliations; the section
partitioner inserted ## Abstract mid-affiliation-block, so the masthead strip
(which stops AT ## Abstract) caught the first two but left the third orphaned in
the Abstract body, and the >=3-consecutive-line _strip_body_affiliation_block
could not reach a single surviving affiliation line.

Fix: _strip_abstract_zone_affiliation_remnant removes an affiliation line (+ a
joint-authors / corresponding-author companion note) that is the FIRST non-blank
content after ## Abstract. A real Abstract always opens with PROSE, so an
affiliation LINE in that slot is unambiguously a boundary-split front-matter
remnant -- the structural signature is affiliation-grammar (via the existing
_is_affiliation_line) at the Abstract-heading boundary, never paper identity. An
abstract that merely MENTIONS a university mid-sentence is prose, not an
affiliation line, and is kept.

Verification (AI-gold via article-finder): chandrashekar's ## Abstract now goes
directly to the abstract body; all 3 affiliations are in the front matter (stripped
by design), none in the Abstract. An abstract-zone FP-scan across the rendered
corpus fires ONLY on chandrashekar -- every other paper's abstract is byte-identical.
4 new tests (incl. real-abstract + university-mention FP guards). Full suite: 1896
passed, only the 2 documented pre-existing whitespace_cells failures remain.

Resolves chandrashekar's METADATA-LEAK (defect #1 of the 2026-07-04 report). Its
Tables 9/10 RC-T data-loss (defects #2/#3) is the architectural cluster --
diagnosed with a precise cycle plan in
docs/FINDINGS_2026-07-04_chandrashekar_rc_t_tables_9_10.md (the missing piece: wire
tables/whitespace.py::char_whitespace_cells to REPLACE a degenerate matched Camelot
table in extract_structured.py::_pick_better_table). Deferred to a dedicated gated
cycle (full 48-paper guard-diff + 7-canary AI-verify; do NOT rush at session-tail).

recent_commits.message: fix(normalize): strip Title-Case surname running header (efendic) + v2.4.114
recent_commits.body:
The independent Sonnet canary audit found efendic_2022_affect leaking its Sage
running header "Efendić et al." at 5 page breaks (the line arrives as
"\fEfendić et al.", splitting a sentence at one). The existing P0r pattern only
stripped an ALL-CAPS "SMITH et al." header; a Title-Case surname was deliberately
excluded because "Smith et al." can be an inline citation.

Fix: a P0r pattern that strips a Title-Case (mixed-case, accented) surname +
"et al." ONLY when it is the COMPLETE line (^...$, after .strip() removes the
leading form-feed) with NOTHING after "et al." -- an inline citation always
carries a "(YEAR)" or continues the sentence ("...by Efendić et al., 2022, this
holds"; "Efendić et al. (2022) found..."), never a bare "Surname et al." alone on
its own line. Surname chars include Latin-1 + Latin Extended-A (ć/ń/ø/š/ž...);
up to 3 tokens with nobiliary particles (van/der/de/...). NORMALIZATION_VERSION
-> 1.9.42.

Verification (running headers are an allowed_omissions category): efendic's 5
"Efendić et al." running-header lines are gone and the sentence they split
reconnects, while all 42 inline "et al." citations (Finucane et al. (2000),
Lerner et al., 2015, ...) are preserved. A 12-case FP battery (6 headers stripped,
6 inline citations kept) + a corpus scan confirm the pattern only matches
standalone running-header lines, never a reference entry or inline citation. 3 new
tests (13 cases). Full suite: 1892 passed, only the 2 documented pre-existing
whitespace_cells failures remain.

This is the 7th fix of the 2026-07-03/04 run 2 (v2.4.108->114); resolves efendic's
running-header METADATA-LEAK. Remaining efendic findings (two demoted subsection
headings, two ×-as-3 residuals) + RC-T + ar_apa figure + whitespace_cells are
queued in docs/HANDOFF_2026-07-04_iterate_run2_seven_fixes.md.

recent_commits.message: fix(render): strip Sage volume/issue + ©-glyph masthead (efendic) + v2.4.113
recent_commits.body:
The independent Sonnet canary audit found efendic_2022_affect leaking its full
Sage masthead between the H1 title and ## Abstract:
  Social Psychological and / Personality Science / 2022, Vol. 13(7) 1173-1184 /
  Ó The Author(s) 2021 / <author byline>
_strip_frontmatter_masthead_block fires only with >=2 hard markers in the
H1->first-## zone, but three Sage shapes were missing from
_looks_like_masthead_hard_marker:
  - the journal volume/issue/page line "2022, Vol. 13(7) 1173-1184" (the bare
    NN-NN page-range pattern didn't match the "<year>, Vol. <v>(<i>)" prefix);
  - the copyright line "Ó The Author(s) 2021" -- © glyph-corrupted to "Ó";
  - the author byline with a "*" corresponding-author mark + trailing comma.

Fix: add _MASTHEAD_VOL_ISSUE_RE; broaden _MASTHEAD_COPYRIGHT_RE to the ©-glyph
forms (Ó/Ã/Â before "The Author(s) <year>", plus a bare "The Author(s) <year>");
relax _MASTHEAD_AUTHOR_SUPERSCRIPT_RE for trailing correspondence marks + comma +
accented names. Only two markers need to match, so the whole zone (incl. byline)
is removed. Still gated on the H1->first-## zone with >=2 markers, so contained.

Verification (masthead is an allowed_omissions category, stripped by design):
efendic renders H1 -> ## Abstract with no masthead furniture between; H1 +
Abstract intact. A wide top-of-document HEAD-vs-fix diff across ~30 corpus papers
changes ONLY efendic (every other top byte-identical). New unit tests assert the
markers fire on the Sage shapes and NOT on body prose with years/numbers
("We ran three studies in 2021 and 2022 with 1184 participants", "Table 3.
Regression coefficients for the 2022 sample"). Full suite: 1879 passed, only the
2 documented pre-existing whitespace_cells failures remain.

Resolves efendic's METADATA-LEAK masthead finding; its remaining findings

recent_commits.message: fix(normalize): recover ×-as-3 in body prose / flattened captions (W0k) + v2.4.112
recent_commits.body:
The independent Sonnet canary audit found efendic_2022_affect's interaction terms
rendering with a corrupted '3' for '×' in body prose + a flattened italic caption
run -- the table-cell-scoped W0i (v2.4.103) never saw these channels (the third
glyph-fixes-need-all-three-text-channels gap on efendic, after 2-for-minus W0j).
  "the three-way interaction (Direction 3 Manipulated Attribute 3 CMA)"
  "*... Direction 3 manipulated attribute PMA 3direction PMA 3 manipulated ...*"

Fix: recover_times_interaction_glyph_in_prose. The '3' is the single most
dangerous prose glyph, so W0k fires ONLY under a tight signature (ALL hold): line
has "interaction" OR >=2 `Word 3 Word` pairs; the 3 is not preceded/left-flanked
by a reference word (Table/Model/Study/Wave/...); the right flank is not a plural
COUNT noun (3 studies/3 groups); >=1 flank is Title-Case or an all-caps acronym
(predictor name Direction/CMA/PMA). A 2-pass relaxation recovers a
lowercase-lowercase pair inside a line that already resolved a '×' (a confirmed
interaction-term run -- the flattened caption). Wired channel 1 + channel 3.

Verification (AI-gold via article-finder): efendic recovers 13 interaction-term '×'
across the captions + body parenthetical + single Title-Case pairs; idempotent. A
21-case FP battery (Table 3 / Study 3 / osf.io/pg3ae / "ran 3 studies with 3
conditions" / "interaction between age and 3 groups" / "participants (n=3) in 2
waves" / "top 3 items on 5 scales") + a 25-paper wide render scan + the
whole-corpus normalize-IDEMPOTENCY gate all clean. The idempotency gate caught a
case a single-pass battery missed: a reference word as the pair's LEFT FLANK
("Model 3 Relationship") -- a later reflow step can move it into a 2-pair line, so
pass-2 fired; fixed by checking the reference-word set against the left flank.
6 new tests (21 cases). Full suite: 1876 passed, only the 2 documented pre-existing
whitespace_cells failures remain.

Two residual efendic cases queued (harder): a line-WRAPPED interaction term and
the 2x2x3 design-notation shape. See
docs/FINDINGS_2026-07-03_efendic_glyph_in_prose_channel.md.

recent_commits.message: fix(render): demote abstract-zone Key-Points finding sentence to body (D3) + v2.4.111
recent_commits.body:
Fixes a PRE-EXISTING failure (test_d3_abstract_zone_no_intermediate_h2, red at
HEAD, not introduced by any recent change -- surfaced when this run's full pytest
ran the whole suite). jama_open_1 rendered "## TRE was more effective for weight
loss" -- a Key-Points FINDING that column-interleaved into the abstract and got
promoted to ##, leaving a stray h2 between ## Abstract and ## Introduction.
_demote_abstract_zone_inline_labels only demoted an explicit allowlist of
structured-abstract LABELS (IMPORTANCE/RESULTS/...), so a finding SENTENCE slipped.

Fix: _is_abstract_finding_sentence. In the abstract zone only, a ## whose text is
a full CLAUSE with a finite VERB (was/reduced/showed/did/...) is demoted to body.
The finite-verb requirement is the discriminator: it demotes "TRE WAS more
effective for weight loss" but keeps a noun-phrase heading like "Effects of Diet
on Body Weight" / "Comparison of TRE and CR" (lowercase prepositions only, no
verb) -- the exact FP class an "any lowercase word" test would have hit. Scoped to
between ## Abstract and the next body-section h2, so blast radius is tiny.

Verification: jama_open_1's finding renders as body; ## Introduction is now the
first h2 after ## Abstract. All 5 test_jama_open_cluster checks pass (D3 was the
only red one). Abstract-zone FP-scan across the rendered corpus demotes ONLY the
jama_open_1 target. 3 new unit-contract tests lock the finding-vs-noun-phrase
discrimination. Full suite: 1855 passed; pre-existing failures down from 3 to 2
(only the whitespace_cells 0-cell regression remains -- diagnosed + queued as its
own investigation: _region_for_caption returns a prose-contaminated region, either
a region-detect regression or a stale test expectation).

recent_commits.message: fix(render): demote over-promoted Results subsection to ### (C3) + v2.4.110
recent_commits.body:
The 2026-07-03 canary AI-verify found ip_feldman_2025_pspb rendering
"## Prevalence Estimates Associations with WellBeing (Replication)" as a
top-level ## when the gold has it as a ### Results subsection, sibling to
"### Prevalence Estimate Errors" and "### Intensity Estimate Errors". It is 7
words -- above the <=6-word ### promoter -- so the 5-12-word ## major-section
promoter grabbed it one level too high.

Fix: _demote_parallel_prefix_subsection_headings demotes a ## back to ### ONLY
when a ### heading earlier in the same section (not crossing a different ##
body-section boundary) shares a >=2-word stemmed prefix AND then diverges. Keyed
on PARALLEL PREFIX STRUCTURE, not "nearest ## parent is Results" -- the latter
would wrongly demote the "## Discussion" that also has Results as its nearest ##
above; Discussion shares no prefix with any ### sibling so it is never touched. A
divergence guard stops a ### that is identical to / a pure prefix of the ## title
(a duplicate-heading artifact) from demoting the gold-correct ## (collabra.90203
renders both the correct "## Extension: Perceived Impact of Donation" and a
spurious "### Extension:..." duplicate; the ## is left alone).

Verification (AI-gold via article-finder): ip_feldman's subsection now renders ###
matching gold; its ### siblings and the following ## Discussion unchanged. With
this, ip_feldman's ENTIRE heading cluster matches gold (C1 fixed 5 wrapped
headings v2.4.108, C3 fixes the 1 over-promotion) -- only Table 10 (RC-T) remains.
FP-validated across ~35 rendered corpus papers (20 canary/broad-read + 15 fresh
psychology): the ONLY demotion is the ip_feldman target; collabra's duplicate ##
correctly preserved. HEAD-vs-fix diff: only ip_feldman changes (17 byte-identical).
5 new tests fail at HEAD, pass with fix. Full suite: 1851 passed, only the 3
documented pre-existing failures remain (jama D3 + whitespace_cells x2 -- NOT this
change). WellBeing->Well-Being hyphen in this heading is OCR-tier won't-fix.

recent_commits.body:
An independent Sonnet canary AI-verify found efendic_2022_affect still FAIL: the
A1/A2 table-cell glyph fixes (v2.4.102/103) recovered 2-for-minus and x-as-3
INSIDE <td> cells only, leaving the body-prose channel corrupt
(glyph-fixes-need-all-three-text-channels). Two prose shapes carry a 2-for-minus
glyph with NO bracket CI, so W0b/W0d (which pair a value against its CI) could not

  - contrast-coding notes: "direction: 20.5 = low, + 0.5 = high"  (should be -0.5)
  - change M-statistics:   "Mchange = 20.14"  (should be -0.14)

magnitude:

recent_commits.message: fix(glyph): stop dropped-minus recovery sign-flipping SE table cells (efendic) + v2.4.104
recent_commits.body:
The third efendic_2022_affect defect from the cycle-1 canary sweep: every
regression table's standard-error (SE) column rendered some values negative
(Intercept SE = 0.06 -> -0.06), impossible because a standard error is
non-negative.

Root cause (reproduce-before-fix corrected a wrong first hypothesis). The guess
was W0d over-recovering a corrupt 2X.XX SE cell; instrumenting the actual render
path disproved it — the SE cell arrives genuinely 0.06, W0d leaves it, and the
minus is added later by recover_dropped_minus_via_ci_pairing (the DROPPED-minus
recovery). In the "B | SE | CI" row the CI describes the B estimate; once B is
recovered to -0.09, the recovery finds the SE 0.06 (whose negation -0.06 fits
B's CI [-0.21, 0.04] while 0.06 does not) and flips it. But the SE legitimately
falls outside the estimate's CI — it is a different column from the estimate the
CI pairs with.

Fix (A3 guard): in a <td> row, do not flip a bare-positive token when a
signed-negative number (the already-recovered estimate) precedes it — that
earlier negative is the CI's estimate, so this token is a later variance column
(SE/SD), non-negative, and must not be flipped. Scoped to <td> rows via the
B->SE->CI column-order invariant; a prose line keeps the original behavior so a
genuinely-first dropped-minus estimate still recovers (ar_apa body betas
byte-identical). The guard only ever BLOCKS a flip (strictly more conservative),
so it cannot introduce a new sign error.

Bumps: __version__/pyproject 2.4.104, NORMALIZATION_VERSION 1.9.39.

Verification (ground truth = AI multimodal read via article-finder reading golds,
never pdftotext): efendic Camelot-ON — every SE value renders positive (Intercept
0.06/0.10/0.15 matching gold); genuine negative B-coefficients + × interaction
terms (A2) unchanged; independent Sonnet AI-gold re-verify: A3 resolved, 0 new
regressions, affiliation-leak now the ONLY remaining efendic defect. 109
dropped-minus + minus-recovery + idempotence tests pass (incl 4 new A3 tests); 335
broad render/sections/normalization pass; a corpus diff of old-vs-new recovery
over 14 papers' table rows = 0 rows changed (guard blocks only SE over-recoveries).

Milestone: efendic's GLYPH cluster (A1 2-for-minus, A2 ×-for-3, A3 SE-sign) is
complete. Only the mid-Introduction affiliation leak (a METADATA-LEAK, different
layer) remains. Standing corpus verdict: FAIL.

recent_commits.message: fix(glyph): recover ×-as-3 in interaction-term table cells (efendic) + v2.4.103
recent_commits.body:
The cycle-1 AI-gold canary re-verify surfaced a second glyph corruption on efendic_2022_affect: the multiplication sign × (interaction operator) decodes as the literal digit 3 in every predictor name across regression Tables 2-5 — "Direction × manipulated attribute" reads "Direction 3 manipulated attribute", "Pleasure × Arousal × CMA" reads "Pleasure 3 Arousal 3 CMA". Same broken-ToUnicode AdvPS… subset font that maps U+2212 minus to '2' (v2.4.102) and '<' to '\' (v2.4.39). recover_times_interaction_glyph (W0i) is TABLE-CELL SCOPED — wired only into cell_cleaning._html_escape, NEVER normalize_text or the render post-process — because a bare '3' between letters is ambiguous in prose ("Table 3 summarizes", "osf.io/pg3ae") but unambiguous in a Camelot predictor cell. Guards: a reference word before the 3 (Model/Study/Wave/…) keeps a genuine ordinal; the cell must carry a ≥3-letter word; recovery reaches across a wrap break (
/merge placeholder) so a 3-way interaction wrapped mid-cell recovers both operators. CRITICAL guard added after a canary-coverage catch: the first cut converted maier_2023_collabra's hypothesis labels H3a/H3b -> "H × a"/"H × b" (a false positive a 12/18-paper random FP scan had missed). iterate-gate.sh --cycle 2 FAILED on I2 (canary-coverage), forcing a maier render that surfaced it. Fix: require whitespace (or a wrap-break) on at least one side of the 3 — interaction terms have a space; glued single-letter labels (H3a, M3, x3y) do not, so they are excluded. Lesson recorded: a sampled corpus scan is necessary but NOT sufficient; the fixed canary roster is the backstop (project lesson + LEARNINGS). Whole-corpus FP scan ~27,000 Camelot cells across ~30 papers (two seeds) = 0 cells changed. A2 unit + real-PDF suite 12 pass; broad render/sections/normalization 385 pass. recent_commits.message: fix(glyph): recover 2-for-minus in Camelot HTML table cells (efendic B-column) + v2.4.102
recent_commits.body:
W0d recover_minus_via_ci_pairing paired a bracket-less point estimate with the
CI in its same record and recovered the '2'-for-U+2212 minus by the containment
invariant — but Camelot emits each <td> on its own line, so the SE cell between
the B-column estimate and the CI cell pushed the char-gap past the 30-char
bare-bracket proximity cap. The recovery fired in the DISABLE_CAMELOT
unstructured-table channel (CI on the same text line) but silently missed every
negative B-coefficient in the Camelot HTML-table channel — the production
default. efendic Tables 2-5: 27 corrupt cells (`20.09` for `-0.09`), a
silently-wrong statistic. The existing test passed only because it set
DOCPLUCK_DISABLE_CAMELOT=1 (a channel blind spot).

Fix: inside an HTML <tr> columns pair by table geometry, not prose adjacency, so
a bare bracket now uses the relaxed (labeled) proximity, still guarded by
_INDEPENDENT_STAT_BETWEEN_RE (rejects pairing across a different estimate's
column). A prose line (no <td>) keeps the strict 30-char cap. Keyed on <tr>
structure, not paper identity -> general. 27->0 corrupt cells; genuine 2.56
(Direction row, in [2.42,2.69]) preserved by containment; idempotent.

Also (pre-existing, leave-nothing-behind): the independent-stat guard now runs
for EVERY bracket kind, not just labeled. A tight-spaced prose variant
`M=5.37, SD=2.01, t(1827)=1.83, d=0.09 [-1.86,0.04]` is 25 chars from 2.01 to
the bracket (within the cap), so the gap check alone let SD=2.01 wrongly recover
to -.01; the CI is d's and t/d intervene -> now rejected.

Bumps: __version__/pyproject 2.4.102, NORMALIZATION_VERSION 1.9.37.

Verification (ground truth = AI multimodal read via article-finder reading golds,
never pdftotext): efendic Camelot-ON 27->0 corrupt B-cells; independent Sonnet
AI-gold re-verify confirms A1 resolved, B-column exact, 0 new regressions. Full
minus suite 22 pass (6 new); adversarial over-recovery battery all pass; broad
normalize/render/sections/heading 557 pass; flatten 39; RC-T degenerate-table
(Camelot) 8; deterministic DISABLE_CAMELOT body-text diff of 8 corpus papers = 0
changed (surgical).

docs/TRIAGE_2026-07-03_head_v2.4.101_assessment.md). This ships ONE root-cause
class (A1); efendic still FAILs on A2 (x-for-3), A3 (SE-spurious-minus), and an

recent_commits.message: fix(tables): v2.4.100 — bmc_med_3 duplicate-fragment pairing (narrow duplicate-starvation rescue)
recent_commits.body:
Resolves the hard case deferred in v2.4.99. On a page with >=2 table captions,
Camelot's stream sometimes emits two near-identical fragments of one table; the
greedy _find_caption_for_table loop handed the duplicate second copy to the
second caption, starving (dropping) that caption's own real table. bmc_med_3 p8:
Table 3's real 11x6 "Comparisons of SCE" grid was replaced by a second copy of
Table 2's 29x5.

Fix: _rescue_duplicate_starved_captions — a post-pass that fires ONLY on the
duplicate signature (>=2 captions assigned identical-raw_text tables). The
best-overlap caption keeps the shared table; each starved caption is reassigned
to the best UNASSIGNED same-page table that fits it at least as well as the
duplicate. Duplicates are kept as separate assignable tables, so a genuine
two-caption/two-identical-grid page (bmc_med_4) still fills both captions
unchanged. bmc_med_3 Table 3 recovers its real 11x6 grid (method gains
dup_rescue:1), AI-gold-verified cell-for-cell against the source page.

Narrow by design: a full global max-token-overlap matching also fixes bmc_med_3
but reshuffled ~24 papers and regressed chan_feldman T6 (a prose fragment
promoted to a caption) — rejected. Keying on the duplicate signature alone
leaves every other page's greedy assignment byte-identical; a whole-corpus
dup_rescue-firing scan confirms the new code path activates on bmc_med_3 alone.
Camelot run-to-run non-determinism defeats naive before/after fingerprint
diffing, so surgicality is proven at the code-path level (no dup_rescue ->
identical HEAD path), not by comparing flaky shapes.

Bumps __version__/pyproject 2.4.99->2.4.100, TABLE_EXTRACTION_VERSION 2.4.5->2.4.6.

Geographic origin, Gender, Ethnic group). Root-caused to _extract_table_body_text's

(tmp/rct_body_text_diff.py) shows the walk is SHARED across every table's raw_text
extraction: the fix shifts body_start on 310 captions (308 recover legitimately,

_new_commits.message: fix(glyph): recover 2 residual ×-as-3 prose shapes W0k can't reach (efendic) + v2.4.116
_new_commits.body:
W0l — the two ×-as-3 glyph residuals the single-line W0k word-pair regex
structurally cannot reach (both silently render `3` where a scientist should
read `×`), documented in docs/FINDINGS_2026-07-03_efendic_glyph_in_prose_channel.md:

(A) recover_times_design_notation — factorial-design notation `<digit>(…) 3
    <digit>(…)`: "2 (Between-subject factor--Direction: …) 3 2 (…) 3 3
    (Within-subject factor--…) mixed-subject design" → each `) 3 <digit> (`
    boundary's `3` is a corrupted `×` (trailing digit is the real factor SIZE).
    Fires ONLY when tied to a genuine factor parenthetical: an adjacent
    parenthetical names a factor type, OR both sides are factor-size parens
    closed by a factorial/design tail. A range recode `(min 1) 3 2 (max 5)`,
    a formula `f(x) 3 2 (y)`, `(from level 3) 3 2 (see note)` are left alone.

(B) recover_times_wrapped_interaction — a line ending `<Pred> 3` whose next
    line starts with a predictor word, inside an "interaction" context:
    "(Direction 3\nManipulated Attribute × CMA)" → "Direction × Manipulated…".
    Reference-word tail guard (FIGURE 3 / Table 3 / Model 3) — the wide FP
    scan caught amj_1 `FIGURE 3\nRegression…` + jamison `Table 3\nMain-effects
    and interactions.` wrongly joined; guard added + regression-tested.

NORMALIZATION_VERSION 1.9.42 → 1.9.43. Wired channel 1 (normalize_text) AND
channel 3 (render post-process) per glyph-fixes-need-all-three-text-channels.

Verification (ground truth = AI multimodal reading golds via article-finder):
- Independent Sonnet canary audit CONFIRMS both efendic glyph fixes correct
  (design `× 2 × 3`, interaction `Direction × Manipulated Attribute × CMA`),
  no ×-as-3 / minus-as-2 corruption anywhere else in efendic.
- Wide 101-PDF raw-text FP scan CLEAN (0 non-target FPs; efendic recovered).
- 26-paper baseline 26/26 PASS. Normalize idempotency corpus PASS.
- 42 W0k/W0l tests + real-PDF end-to-end (fails at HEAD v2.4.115, passes here).

Resolves efendic's two ×-as-3 residuals. Remaining efendic findings (2 demoted
subsection headings; a NEW Table 4/5 mid-sentence splice) are queued; RC-T
table-data-loss on ip_feldman/plos_med/chandrashekar remains architectural.

_new_commits.message: fix(render): strip affiliation remnant orphaned in Abstract body (chandrashekar) + v2.4.115
_new_commits.body:
A canary AI-verify found chandrashekar_2023_mp rendering "Department of Philosophy,
Lake Forest College" + "*Joint first authors" right after the ## Abstract heading,
before the abstract prose. The paper has 3 author affiliations; the section
partitioner inserted ## Abstract mid-affiliation-block, so the masthead strip
(which stops AT ## Abstract) caught the first two but left the third orphaned in
the Abstract body, and the >=3-consecutive-line _strip_body_affiliation_block
could not reach a single surviving affiliation line.

Fix: _strip_abstract_zone_affiliation_remnant removes an affiliation line (+ a
joint-authors / corresponding-author companion note) that is the FIRST non-blank
content after ## Abstract. A real Abstract always opens with PROSE, so an
affiliation LINE in that slot is unambiguously a boundary-split front-matter
remnant -- the structural signature is affiliation-grammar (via the existing
_is_affiliation_line) at the Abstract-heading boundary, never paper identity. An
abstract that merely MENTIONS a university mid-sentence is prose, not an
affiliation line, and is kept.

Verification (AI-gold via article-finder): chandrashekar's ## Abstract now goes
directly to the abstract body; all 3 affiliations are in the front matter (stripped
by design), none in the Abstract. An abstract-zone FP-scan across the rendered
corpus fires ONLY on chandrashekar -- every other paper's abstract is byte-identical.
4 new tests (incl. real-abstract + university-mention FP guards). Full suite: 1896
passed, only the 2 documented pre-existing whitespace_cells failures remain.

Resolves chandrashekar's METADATA-LEAK (defect #1 of the 2026-07-04 report). Its
Tables 9/10 RC-T data-loss (defects #2/#3) is the architectural cluster --
diagnosed with a precise cycle plan in
docs/FINDINGS_2026-07-04_chandrashekar_rc_t_tables_9_10.md (the missing piece: wire
tables/whitespace.py::char_whitespace_cells to REPLACE a degenerate matched Camelot
table in extract_structured.py::_pick_better_table). Deferred to a dedicated gated
cycle (full 48-paper guard-diff + 7-canary AI-verify; do NOT rush at session-tail).

You provide your own API key and pay the LLM provider directly — typically **under $2 per review**.

Don't want to run it locally? Use the [web interface](https://coarse.vercel.app/) instead.


Get an API key from [OpenRouter](https://openrouter.ai/keys) (free to sign up), then:

```bash
uvx coarse-ink review paper.pdf --api-key sk-or-v1-YOUR_KEY
```

That's it. The review is written to `paper_review.md` in the current directory.

repos.readme_excerpt:

- `templates/` + `static/`: Frontend pages

+  variables. The dedup

-0.5.11. Development has been active across 0.2.4-0.5.11 -- new test types,
+0.6.14. Development has been active across 0.2.4-0.6.14 -- new test types,

 many parser and consistency fixes -- and the most significant change is
 structural, in 0.4.0 (see "Breaking change" below). Every intervening version

+  "schemaVersion": 1,
+  "requiredFields": [
+    "source",
+    "test_type",
+    "stat_value",
+    "df1",

+    "p_reported",
+    "p_computed",
+    "status",
+    "decision_error"
+  ],
+  "statuses": ["PASS", "OK", "NOTE", "WARN", "ERROR", "SKIP"],
+  "testTypes": [
+    "t", "F", "r", "chisq", "z", "U", "W", "H", "regression",
+    "spearman", "kendall", "kendall_w", "dscf", "cochran_q", "binomial",
+    "RR", "rdpct", "md_hl", "interaction_p", "mediation_i

 library(testthat)
 library(effectcheck)
 
-test_check("effectcheck")
+# R CMD check evaluates this file with R CMD BATCH on Windows.  Make the
+# runner's outcome explicit: testthat prints a useful summary, while this
+# process exits successfully only when every expectation did.  This avoids a
+# platform-dependent non-zero BATCH exit after an otherwise clean suite.
+results <- test_check("effectcheck", reporter = "summary", stop_on_failure = FALSE)
+result_table <- as.data.fr

@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+test_that("the versioned result contract matches check_text output", {
+  fixture_path <- system.file("contracts", "analysis-result.fixture.json", package = "effectcheck")
+  expect_true(nzchar(fixture_path))
+  fixture <- jsonlite::fromJSON(fixture_path)
+  result <- check_text("The effect was significant, t(48) = 2.31, p = .025, d = 0.66.")
+
+  expect_true(all(fixture$requiredFields %in% names(result)),
+              info = "check_text() dropped a required worker/frontend c

@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+# v0.6.14 (E-corr-two-prose): the v0.5.14 body-vs-table-fragment r-dedup must NOT
+# collapse two DISTINCT prose correlations that coincidentally share the same r,
+# df, and N and carry no CI to tell them apart.
+#
+# The v0.5.14 dedup was built to collapse a body-text correlation (`r(741) = -.43,
+# 95% CI [...]`, parenthesized) against its TABLE-FRAGMENT restatement (`r = -.43
+# [...]`, no parenthesized `r(df)` form). Its key is (test_type, stat, df1, df2, N,
+# ciL, ciU, e

A metadata and text extraction and text manipulation tool set for the statistical programming language [R](www.r-project.org). 

**JATSdecoder** facilitates text mining projects on scientific articles by enabling an individual selection of metadata and text parts. 
Its function `JATSdecoder()` extracts metadata, sectioned text and reference list from [NISO-JATS](https://jats.nlm.nih.gov/publishing/tag-library/1.1d2/index.html) coded XML files. 
The function `study.character()` uses the `JATSdecoder()` result to perform fine-tuned text extraction tasks to identify key study characteristics like

Auto Z-Curve reads a folder of PDF articles, uses Google's Gemini AI to extract focal statistical results from each one, and produces a z-curve analysis report — including a plot, summary statistics, and a full disclosure table.

You interact with it through a simple terminal interface: point it at your PDFs, enter your API key, and click Run.

In the below screenshot, I z-curved 45 articles on cognitive dissonance in under 4 minutes, costing under 2 USD in tokens.

![](assets/example.svg)

**Note:** I have validated statistic extraction as part of a study, which is currently under peer review

r_meta.title: Automated Reproducibility Checks for R Markdown Documents
r_meta.description_text: Provide reproducible R chunks in R Markdown document that automatically check computational results for reproducibility. This is achieved by creating json files storing metadata about computational results. A comprehensive tutorial to the package is available as preprint by Brandmaier & Peikert (2024, <doi:10.31234/osf.io/3zjvf>).

repos.readme_excerpt:
Also read our Open Access publication about this package in _Collabra: Psychology_:
[Automated Reproducibility Testing in R Markdown](https://doi.org/10.1525/collabra.138638)


This package allows you to make computational results in R testable for reproduction (does the same script with the same data produce the same results, e.g. on a different computer and/or later in time). There is only a single thing you need to change in your analysis if you are already using RMarkdown: Load the package at the beginning of your R Markdown file (`library(reproducibleRchunks)`) and change the code chunk t

repos.readme_excerpt:
OIF (Output It Forward) is a Chrome extension that helps researchers detect and document open data indicators in scholarly articles while they browse. As you read a paper, OIF automatically scans the page for phrases and repository links commonly associated with open data practices, and logs matches to a running CSV file for systematic documentation.
OIF is designed with adaptability in mind: its phrase list and repository domain list are plain, editable text files, so tailoring the extension to your discipline requires no programming expertise, just edit a list.

Follow this link: https://chr

digest.narrative: Our open-source research community has seen significant progress across multiple projects. Notably, the `statcheck` and `escicheck` projects, both led by MicheleNuijten and Gilad Feldman respectively, have made administrative updates and synchronized their main branches with recent changes. Meanwhile, the `oddpub` project has updated its ontogenerator's code validation process, and `docpluck` has addressed several issues, including text loss and rendering fixes. The `coarse` project has re-enabled Fable 5 in the model picker, and `regcheck` has resolved multiple bugs, including issues with its demo page, card preview, and ad blocker interference. These connections between projects highlight the collaborative spirit of our community, and we celebrate the progress made towards improving research tools and methodologies.
